rationale: The baseline remains the strongest training recipe, so this change leaves optimization and architecture intact and attacks the actual scoring surface: the final serialized roundtrip. This script now does a budget-aware mixed-precision export that keeps all float tensors as fp16/fp32 passthrough whenever the model comfortably fits under the 16MB artifact cap, and only falls back to the older int8 path if it would not fit. On this 5.8M-parameter baseline, that should preserve much more of the trained model at final evaluation with essentially no training-risk and a plausible win over the current quantized roundtrip.
title: Budget-Aware FP16 Roundtrip Serialization
